threats.title: Microsoft's MDASH AI System Finds 16 Windows Flaws Fixed in Patch Tuesday
threats.summary: Microsoft has unveiled a new multi-model artificial intelligence (AI)-driven system called MDASH to facilitate vulnerability discovery and remediation at scale, adding that it's being tested by some customers as part of a limited private preview. threats.title: Azerbaijani Energy Firm Hit by Repeated Microsoft Exchange Exploitation
threats.summary: A threat actor with affiliations to China has been linked to a "multi-wave intrusion" targeting an unnamed Azerbaijani oil and gas company between late December 2025 and late February 2026, marking an expansion of its targeting. threats.title: Most Remediation Programs Never Confirm the Fix Actually Worked
threats.summary: Security teams have never had better visibility into their environments and never been worse at confirming what they fix stays fixed. Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report puts the mean time to exploit at an estimated negative seven days. The Verizon 2025 DBIR puts median time to remediate edge device vulnerabilities at 32 days. threats.title: Microsoft Patches 138 Vulnerabilities, Including DNS and Netlogon RCE Flaws
threats.summary: Microsoft on Tuesday released patches for 138 security vulnerabilities spanning its product portfolio, although none of them have been listed as publicly known or under active attack. Of the 138 flaws, 30 are rated Critical, 104 are rated Important, three are rated Moderate, and one is rated Low in severity.
